text: Abstract Abstract In order to increase the character-building process in SMKN 1 Masbagik students, this study examines the applicability of Islamic Education values incorporated into the Strengthening Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture (P5BK) program through activities carried out in the Masbagik Vocational School, Canggar, Depok District, Special Region of Yogyakarta. The method used is qualitative descriptive. Data collection was carried out by interviewing, through direct field observation and data documentation and is processed further using the Miles and Huberman interactive analysis model. The results of the study show that Islamic Education values are distributed across all three dimensions of the P5BK program, namely the dimensions of sustainable life, employability, and local wisdom, in each of the four stages of planning, action, evaluation and supporting factors. Through the integrated implementation of Islamic Education values in the P5BK program for students at SMKN 1 Masbagik, the following advantages are obtained, namely flexibility in the learning process, close interaction between schools and the industrial community and students receive direct learning experience to increase their environmental awareness and social awareness. This research also confirms theoretically that the P5BK program can increase teacher pedagogical competence and carry out their task optimally and increase the quality of vocational education carried out in schools, in line with the Pendidikan Merdeka (Independent Learning) curriculum. Keywords: Development of Character Building in Vocational Schools, P5BK Program, SMKN 1 Masbagik, Integrated Implementation of Islamic Education Values.
